title: Atlas master config v1
description: The single config file that tells the atlas registry where everything lives on this machine. Validated at atlas startup. Adding a new data domain = add a root + add layer entries that reference it. Moving to a different machine = edit paths here, layers stay unchanged.

workspace_root.description: Location of this config file. Relative roots are resolved against it. '.' means the current directory.

active_atlas.description: Which atlas the shell loads on startup. Optional; defaults to 'inversion'.

active_chrom.description: Default chromosome on first page load. Optional.

active_cohort.description: Cohort_id the registry resolves {cohort_id} against for cohort_scoped roots, and through which {genome_id} is resolved (via the cohort's genome_id FK) for genome_scoped roots. Optional for atlases that don't yet use the cohort/genome scoping flags. Per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md.

species.description: List of species this atlas instance knows about. Each entry references a species.config.yaml file declaring species_id, cohort, populations, and reference. Single-species atlases list one entry. Multi-species atlases list all known species with exactly one marked active=true (the default focus); the others remain loadable for cross-species analyses.

id.description: Species ID. Must match the species_id field inside the referenced species.config.yaml. Used as the {species_id} slot in templated paths.

config.description: Path to the species.config.yaml file (relative to workspace_root, or absolute).

active.description: True for the species the atlas defaults to focusing on. At most one species should be active=true; if more than one is true, the first wins. If none is true, the first listed species is used.

roots.description: Named root directories the registry routes to. Each layer entry references a root by name (e.g. root: 'cohort_relatedness') rather than by literal path. Roots may declare one of three mutually-exclusive scoping flags: species_scoped (legacy, single {species_id} substitution), cohort_scoped (current — {cohort_id} substitution per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md), or genome_scoped (current — {genome_id} substitution). Per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md the cohort/genome flags supersede species_scoped; species_scoped is retained for per-page migration back-compat. At most one of the three may be true on a single root.

server.description: popstats_server.py settings. Optional — server is not required for the atlas to load (file-source layers work without it).

engines.description: Paths to compiled engine binaries (region_popstats, hobs_windower, angsd_patched, instant_q). null disables that engine. Server reads this section.

defaults.description: Per-engine default parameters: popstats / hobs / angsd. Optional; engines fall back to hardcoded defaults if absent.

path.description: Filesystem path to this root. May be relative (resolved against atlas.workspace_root) or absolute. ${roots.X.path} substitution is supported. The path may contain at most one templating slot resolved at fetch time: {species_id} when species_scoped is true, {cohort_id} when cohort_scoped is true, {genome_id} when genome_scoped is true.

role.description: Which 4-role namespace this root contributes to. Layers under this root inherit the namespace by default.

species_scoped.description: LEGACY (per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md §'Where data physically lives', superseded by cohort_scoped / genome_scoped). True = the root's path contains {species_id} which the registry templates from the active species (or args.species_id) at fetch time. Retained for per-page migration back-compat; new roots should use cohort_scoped or genome_scoped instead. Mutually exclusive with cohort_scoped and genome_scoped.

cohort_scoped.description: True = the root's path contains {cohort_id} which the registry templates from the active cohort (or args.cohort_id) at fetch time. Use for roots whose contents are cohort-scoped per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md: relatedness, ancestry, popstats, candidates, groups. The active cohort_id is read from master_config.atlas.active_cohort (or args). Mutually exclusive with species_scoped and genome_scoped.

genome_scoped.description: True = the root's path contains {genome_id} which the registry templates from the active cohort's genome_id (or args.genome_id) at fetch time. Use for roots whose contents are genome-scoped per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md: precomp, dosage (anything tied to a specific assembly's coordinates). Resolved via the active cohort's FK to genome. Mutually exclusive with species_scoped and cohort_scoped.

writable.description: True = Registry.write may target paths under this root (combined with the layer entry's writable flag and the server's path allowlist). Read-only data roots (precomp, cohort, beagle, bams, reference) leave this false.

ephemeral.description: True = contents are safe to delete; the registry expects to find them missing and rebuild on demand. cache root is the only ephemeral root by default.

excluded_from_tarballs.description: True = exclude this root from project tarballs by convention. Set on the cache root so test-run accumulation doesn't bloat handoff packages.

description.description: Free-form human-readable description of what this root contains.

files.description: Optional: named files within this root that other parts of the system reference by name (e.g. reference.files.fasta = 'fClaHyb_Gar_LG.fa').

bam_suffix.description: Only meaningful for the bams root. Suffix used for per-sample BAM resolution: '<sample>.<bam_suffix>' with fallback to '.bam' / '.markdup.bam'.
